keys.effect: TAA sample count and temporal stability: 0=low, 1=medium, 2=high, 3=epic, 4=cinematic
Shadow cascade count, resolution, and soft-shadow quality: 0-4
Texture streaming quality: 0=low, 1=medium, 2=high, 3=epic, 4=cinematic
keys.notes: Bandwidth-bound rather than GPU-compute-bound. Reducing below 2 rarely improves FPS on mid/high-end hardware.

keys.effect: Particle, fire, and environmental effects quality: 0-4
Material shading model complexity, particularly visible on skin and metal in DI2's LA environments: 0-4
Post-process effects quality including bloom and ambient occlusion: 0-4
LOD and object draw distance: 0-4
Foliage density and quality: 0-4
Screen-space reflection fidelity: 0-4
Screen-space ambient occlusion detail: 0-4
Indirect (capsule) shadow quality for characters: 0-4
How many off-screen or distant objects are treated as high-significance and avoid quality cutbacks: 0-4
Enable or disable in-game VSync
keys.notes: Always disable. Use G-Sync, FreeSync, or driver-level VSync to avoid input latency.

keys.effect: Maximum frame rate cap in Hz (0 = unlimited)
keys.notes: For VRR displays, set 2-3 fps below monitor maximum (e.g. 237 for a 240 Hz panel) to keep inside the VRR range and prevent VSync engaging.

keys.effect: Enable HDR output
keys.notes: Set True only if HDR is enabled at the OS/display level. Setting True on an SDR display causes washed-out colours. The agent should check the display's HDR state before recommending.

keys.effect: Motion blur quality: 0=off, 1-4=increasing quality
keys.notes: Recommended off in all goals. Primary motion sickness trigger with no meaningful visual benefit in a first-person melee game.

keys.effect: Depth of field quality: 0=off
keys.notes: Recommended off in all goals. Causes eye strain in first-person games and is a motion_comfort trigger.

keys.effect: Maximum chromatic aberration intensity
keys.notes: Recommended off. Peripheral colour fringing is a motion sickness trigger and degrades perceived sharpness.

keys.effect: Enable or disable film grain overlay
keys.notes: Recommended off. Persistent visual noise increases perceptual fatigue with no quality benefit.

keys.effect: Enable or disable engine lens flare bloom halos around bright light sources
keys.notes: Recommended off. Bright flash halos are a photosensitivity and motion_comfort trigger.

keys.effect: Anisotropic filtering level (1-16)
keys.notes: AF is essentially free on modern GPUs. 16x is recommended for balanced and quality goals.

keys.effect: Enable Hierarchical Z-Buffer GPU occlusion culling
keys.notes: Always enable. Reduces GPU load by culling occluded geometry before shading.

keys.effect: Texture streaming pool size in MB. Larger pools reduce texture pop-in.
keys.notes: Scale with VRAM: ~1000 for 4-6 GB, ~4096 for 8-10 GB, ~8000 for 12 GB+. At 5120x1440 the larger pool is especially important.

keys.effect: Minimum apparent radius for an object to cast a dynamic shadow. Lower = more objects cast shadows.
Maximum shadow map resolution per light
Resolution of baked reflection captures — visible in interiors and on glossy surfaces

engine_overrides.behaviour: Scalability group values (sg.ShadowQuality, sg.TextureQuality, etc.) written to GameUserSettings.ini may be overwritten when the player adjusts any setting in the in-game Graphics menu.
engine_overrides.action: Safe to set via INI, but warn the user that in-game Graphics menu changes will overwrite them. For persistent overrides, use Engine.ini CVars directly.

engine_overrides.behaviour: Dead Island 2 ships FSR 2.1 natively. A known bug causes the game to crash when opening the inventory menu while FSR 2 is enabled.
engine_overrides.action: Always recommend keeping FSR 2 off. Flag this bug if the user mentions FSR or asks about upscaling.

manual_only_settings.reason: Below ~80° increases nausea risk in first-person games. 90–95° is the motion_comfort sweet spot without introducing edge distortion.
Walking and combat head bob is a strong vestibular trigger in first-person melee games.
Hit-impact camera jolts compound with fast melee combat to increase motion discomfort.

notes: Dead Island 2 uses UE4 4.25 with a 0-4 scalability range on most sg.* keys, unlike the standard UE4 0-3 range. Recommendations account for this.
At 5120x1440 (ultrawide) or higher resolutions, increase r.Streaming.PoolSize above the UE4 engine default — texture pop-in is more visible at high resolutions.
Native FSR 2.1 is present but should be left off due to the inventory crash bug. At 1440p and above on RTX 3070+ hardware, native rendering is preferred anyway.
HDR output is supported. Only set bUseHDRDisplayOutput=True if the OS display HDR toggle is also on.
